text:
COMPUTED TOMOGRAPHY OF THE CHEST, ABDOMEN, AND PELVIS WITH INTRAVENOUS CONTRAST
MRI BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT GADOLINIUM CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 48-year-old female with newly diagnosed acute myeloid leukemia, FLT3-ITD positive, presenting with WBC 87,400/uL, circulating blasts, anemia, and thrombocytopenia. Staging imaging requested prior to initiation of induction chemotherapy. Evaluate for organomegaly, lymphadenopathy, extramedullary disease, pleural or pericardial effusions, and CNS involvement.

TECHNIQUE:
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis: Multidetector CT performed in the portal venous phase following administration of 100 mL of Omnipaque 350 intravenous contrast material. Axial, coronal, and sagittal reconstructions reviewed at standard soft tissue and lung window settings. Coverage from the lung apices through the symphysis pubis.

MRI brain: Standard brain MRI protocol performed on a 3.0 Tesla magnet. Sequences include pre-contrast axial T1, axial T2, axial FLAIR, axial DWI with ADC mapping, axial T2* GRE (susceptibility-weighted), and post-contrast axial and coronal T1 with fat saturation following administration of 0.1 mmol/kg gadolinium-based contrast agent (Gadavist). No prior brain imaging available for direct comparison.

COMPARISON: No prior cross-sectional imaging available.

---

CT CHEST FINDINGS:

Lungs and Airways: Bilateral small to moderate pleural effusions are present, greater on the right than the left, with associated compressive atelectasis at both lung bases. The right pleural effusion layers along the posterior and lateral pleural space and measures approximately 1.5 cm in maximal depth in the dependent position; the left is somewhat smaller at approximately 1.0 cm. No evidence of loculation. There is mild bandlike subsegmental atelectasis in the bilateral lower lobes posteriorly. The remainder of the lung parenchyma is clear. No focal consolidations, masses, or pulmonary nodules identified. No interstitial edema or ground-glass opacification to suggest diffuse alveolar injury. The airways are patent to the subsegmental level bilaterally.

Mediastinum: The heart is normal in size. No pericardial effusion. The aorta and great vessels are normal in caliber and course. No evidence of mediastinal widening. No anterior or posterior mediastinal masses or adenopathy. The thymus is normal in appearance for patient age. Esophagus is unremarkable.

Lymph Nodes: No pathologically enlarged mediastinal or hilar lymph nodes. Scattered subcentimeter mediastinal nodes (prevascular, paratracheal, subcarinal stations) are noted, all measuring below 1 cm in short axis dimension, within normal limits. No hilar adenopathy.

Chest Wall and Bony Thorax: No lytic, blastic, or mixed osseous lesions involving the ribs, sternum, or visible thoracic spine. No cortical destruction. Bone marrow attenuation is not reliably assessed on contrast-enhanced CT; however, there are no focal destructive lesions.

---

CT ABDOMEN AND PELVIS FINDINGS:

Liver: The liver is mildly but diffusely enlarged, measuring approximately 17.1 cm in the greatest craniocaudal dimension along the midclavicular line. Hepatic parenchymal enhancement is homogeneous throughout all hepatic lobes without focal hypodense or hyperdense lesions. The hepatic contour is smooth. The intrahepatic bile ducts are not dilated. The main portal vein and hepatic veins are patent.

Spleen: The spleen is enlarged, measuring approximately 14.2 cm in the greatest craniocaudal dimension (upper limit of normal typically considered approximately 12 cm). The splenic parenchyma enhances homogeneously without focal lesions, splenic infarcts, or nodular deposits. The splenic vein is patent. The degree of splenomegaly is mild to moderate.

Pancreas: Normal size, morphology, and enhancement. No ductal dilation. No peripancreatic stranding or fluid.

Kidneys and Adrenal Glands: Bilateral kidneys are normal in size with symmetric and brisk cortical enhancement. No hydronephrosis. No solid renal masses or nephrolithiasis. The adrenal glands are normal bilaterally.

Gastrointestinal Tract: Stomach is decompressed and unremarkable. The small bowel loops demonstrate normal fold pattern and caliber. Colon is without focal wall thickening or masses. The appendix is visualized and is normal.

Lymph Nodes: No retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or pelvic lymphadenopathy. Scattered subcentimeter retroperitoneal nodes are present, all below 1 cm in short axis, without pathologic enlargement.

Pelvis: The urinary bladder is adequately distended with no focal wall thickening. The uterus is normal in size and morphology. No free pelvic fluid beyond a trace physiologic amount. No pelvic masses.

Peritoneum and Mesentery: No ascites. No peritoneal thickening or nodularity to suggest peritoneal deposits.

Osseous Structures (Abdomen/Pelvis): No lytic or blastic osseous lesions identified within the lumbar spine, sacrum, iliac wings, or proximal femora. No vertebral compression fractures. No pathological bone lesions appreciated.

Vasculature: The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. The celiac axis, superior mesenteric artery, and bilateral iliac vessels are patent.

---

MRI BRAIN FINDINGS:

Parenchyma: The brain parenchyma is normal in signal intensity and morphology on all sequences. The cerebral cortex, subcortical white matter, and deep white matter structures are without focal signal abnormality. No areas of restricted diffusion to suggest acute ischemia. No T2/FLAIR signal hyperintensity in a distribution consistent with encephalitis, demyelination, or prior infarct. The basal ganglia, thalami, brainstem, and cerebellar hemispheres are symmetric and unremarkable.

Extra-axial Spaces and Meninges: No leptomeningeal enhancement is identified on post-contrast sequences. The meninges enhance symmetrically and smoothly without nodularity, thickening, or pachymeningeal deposits. No subdural or epidural collections. No extra-axial masses.

Ventricles and Cisterns: The ventricular system is normal in size and configuration. No hydrocephalus. The basal cisterns are patent.

Post-Contrast Enhancement: Following gadolinium administration, no parenchymal lesions demonstrate abnormal enhancement. No ring-enhancing lesions. No evidence of leptomeningeal carcinomatosis or leukemic meningeal infiltration.

Calvarium and Orbits: No lytic or sclerotic skull lesions. The orbits are symmetric and unremarkable.

Vascular Structures: The major intracranial vessels demonstrate normal flow voids. No gross vascular malformations.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Mild to moderate splenomegaly (14.2 cm craniocaudal) and mild hepatomegaly (17.1 cm craniocaudal), consistent with systemic infiltration in the setting of the patient's known leukemia. No focal splenic or hepatic lesions.

2. Small bilateral pleural effusions, right greater than left, with associated bibasilar compressive atelectasis. No loculation.

3. No mediastinal, hilar, retroperitoneal, or pelvic lymphadenopathy.

4. No evidence of extramedullary disease with discrete soft tissue masses or leukemic chloroma deposits in any imaged compartment.

5. No lytic or blastic osseous lesions. Osseous metastatic involvement cannot be fully excluded by CT alone, though no focal destructive lesions are present.

6. No CNS involvement by MRI brain: no parenchymal lesions, no leptomeningeal enhancement, no restricted diffusion or abnormal signal to suggest leukemic infiltration of the brain or meninges.

Overall, the imaging pattern is compatible with the known diagnosis of acute myeloid leukemia with systemic involvement reflected by organomegaly and reactive pleural effusions. No discrete tumor masses or sites of extramedullary disease are identified.

Interpreting Radiologist: [Attending Radiologist, Division of Body and Neuroradiology]
Facility: [Academic Medical Center]
